statement.markdown:
You are given an array `nums` and an integer `target`. Each element in the array can be added or subtracted to reach the target value. Return `true` if there exists a way to sum up to the target, otherwise, return `false`.

You are not allowed to use any sort operations, and must use dynamic programming to solve this problem.

**Example:**
```
Input: nums = [1, 2, -3, 3], target = 3
Output: true
```
statement.inputFormat:
First line: integer array size `n`
Second line: elements of the array separated by spaces
Third line: target value
statement.outputFormat: `true` if there exists a way to sum up to the target, `false` otherwise

hints.text: Use dynamic programming to store subproblems.

editorial.title: Editorial Title
editorial.intuition: Use a boolean array `dp` to store whether it's possible to reach each value in the array.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use dynamic programming to store subproblems.

functionSignature.java: public boolean canSum(int[] nums, int target);
functionSignature.python: def canSum(self, nums: list[int], target: int) -> bool;
